tagline: Film room intelligence at machine speed.

round_of_64.reasoning: Duke's AdjEM of 37.3 is the best in the field, creating a 39-point efficiency gap over Siena's -1.7. The Blue Devils' AdjO of 128.1 is generational offense — Siena's defense at 108.9 cannot even slow this down. This is the most lopsided matchup in the bracket.

Ohio State holds a significant AdjEM edge (23.6 vs 15.2) driven by a 10-point offensive efficiency gap (AdjO 125.2 vs 114.8). TCU's slightly better defense (99.7 vs 101.6) cannot compensate for such a massive scoring disparity. Ohio State generates too many quality looks for TCU's half-court defense to contain.

St. John's AdjEM of 25.7 creates a 14.6-point edge over Northern Iowa's 11.1. The Johnnies' defense (AdjD 94.1) is elite, and Northern Iowa's slower tempo (62.2) limits their opportunities to generate enough possessions to close the gap. St. John's pace and defensive pressure suffocate mid-major offenses.

Kansas holds a 16.3-point AdjEM advantage (23.5 vs 7.2) with better efficiency on both ends. Bill Self's tournament pedigree at the 4-13 line is nearly automatic. Cal Baptist's AdjD of 100.3 cannot slow Kansas's balanced attack, and the physicality gap in the post is stark.

Louisville's 10.5-point AdjEM edge (25.9 vs 15.4) is decisive for a 6-11 matchup. The Cardinals' AdjO of 124.0 at a fast tempo (69.7) generates scoring in transition that South Florida's porous defense (AdjD 101.4) cannot handle. Louisville's offensive firepower creates separation by the second half.

Michigan State's AdjEM of 26.7 towers over North Dakota State's 5.4 — a 21.3-point gap. Tom Izzo's tournament track record at the 3-14 seed line is nearly flawless. Michigan State's defense (AdjD 96.2) locks down North Dakota State's limited offensive options (AdjO 111.5).

UCLA's 8.5-point AdjEM advantage (22.8 vs 14.3) is solid for a 7-10 game. UCLA's offense (AdjO 124.5) outpaces UCF's (120.0), and their slower tempo (64.7) limits UCF's transition opportunities. UCF's weak defense (AdjD 105.7) means UCLA generates quality looks consistently in the half court.

UConn's AdjEM of 28.1 creates a 30.2-point efficiency gap over Furman's -2.1. The Huskies' defense (AdjD 95.0) will smother Furman's modest offense (AdjO 106.9). Two-time defending champions have the tournament DNA and roster depth to dominate a 15-seed from start to finish.

Florida's AdjEM of 33.8 creates a 43.8-point efficiency gap over Prairie View A&M's -10.0. The Gators' offense (AdjO 126.1) and defense (AdjD 92.3) are both elite, while PVAMU's defense at 111.4 is a sieve. This is an annihilation-level mismatch — Florida's tempo pushes the pace to amplify the talent gap.

Iowa's AdjEM of 21.9 edges Clemson's 19.6, driven by a significantly better offense (AdjO 122.1 vs 116.5). In a slow-tempo 8-9 matchup (both under 65 possessions), the team that generates better half-court offense wins — and Iowa's 5.6-point offensive efficiency advantage is the clear separator.

Vanderbilt's AdjEM of 28.1 is 17.1 points above McNeese's 11.0. The Commodores' explosive offense (AdjO 127.4) creates a 14.2-point scoring gap that McNeese's defense (AdjD 102.2) cannot close. Classic 5-12 upset territory, but Vanderbilt's offensive firepower is too potent for a Southland Conference defense.

Nebraska's AdjEM of 23.8 holds a 22.1-point advantage over Troy's 1.7. Nebraska's elite defense (AdjD 93.5) will strangle Troy's limited offense (AdjO 110.7). The physicality and depth gap between a Big Ten 4-seed and a Sun Belt 13-seed is simply too wide.

North Carolina holds a 5.2-point AdjEM edge (21.4 vs 16.2) with better offense (AdjO 121.5 vs 119.3) and defense (AdjD 100.1 vs 103.1). Both teams play at similar up-tempo paces, but UNC's superior talent and Hubert Davis's tournament experience give the Tar Heels the edge in a fast-paced 6-11 game.

Illinois' AdjEM of 33.7 is elite — a 32.5-point gap over Penn's 1.2. The Illini offense (AdjO 131.9) is the second-best in the tournament behind Duke. Penn's defense (AdjD 105.4) will be overwhelmed by Illinois' scoring volume. This is a blowout from the opening tip.

Texas A&M's faster tempo (71.0 vs 65.3) creates more possessions that neutralize Saint Mary's half-court defensive advantage. While Saint Mary's holds a 3.3-point AdjEM edge (21.8 vs 18.5), Texas A&M's offensive output (AdjO 119.9) at their preferred pace disrupts Saint Mary's slow-grinding game plan. The Aggies' SEC physicality overwhelms the WCC's deliberate style.

Houston's AdjEM of 33.0 creates a 30.4-point gap over Idaho's 2.6. The Cougars' defense (AdjD 92.4) is among the nation's best and will suffocate Idaho's modest offense (AdjO 108.8). Houston's slow tempo (63.3) limits possessions, and their defensive wall makes every Idaho shot a contested battle.

Arizona's AdjEM of 35.5 creates a 41.6-point gap over LIU's -6.1. The Wildcats' defense (AdjD 91.4) is the third-best in the tournament, and their offense (AdjO 126.9) at a fast pace (70.0) will generate transition buckets that LIU's porous defense (AdjD 109.8) cannot contain.

Utah State's AdjEM of 20.9 edges Villanova's 19.1, with a stronger offense (AdjO 123.0 vs 119.6). Utah State's faster tempo (67.6 vs 64.9) creates extra possessions where their offensive efficiency advantage compounds. In a coin-flip 8-9 game, the team generating better looks per possession takes it.

Wisconsin's AdjEM of 25.5 holds a 17.3-point advantage over High Point's 8.2. The Badgers' offense (AdjO 127.2) is explosive, and High Point's defense (AdjD 107.5) is porous. Wisconsin's Big Ten physicality and tournament experience at the 5-12 line give them a comfortable cushion.

Arkansas's AdjEM of 26.3 creates a 21.3-point gap over Hawaii's 5.0. The Razorbacks' explosive offense (AdjO 127.9) at a fast tempo (71.0) will overwhelm Hawaii's modest defensive profile (AdjD 101.8). Hawaii's long travel from the islands adds fatigue to an already steep talent mismatch.

BYU holds a modest 2.7-point AdjEM edge (20.4 vs 17.7) with slightly better offense (AdjO 124.7 vs 124.1) and defense (AdjD 104.3 vs 106.4). In a tight 6-11 matchup at similar tempos, BYU's marginal efficiency advantages on both ends accumulate over 40 minutes.

Gonzaga's AdjEM of 26.3 creates a 25.0-point gap over Kennesaw State's 1.3. The Bulldogs' defense (AdjD 94.0) will lock down Kennesaw State's modest offense (AdjO 110.8). Mark Few's teams don't lose to 14-seeds — Gonzaga's offensive efficiency and tournament pedigree make this straightforward.

Miami's AdjEM edge (19.6 vs 16.9) is modest but supported by slightly better defense (AdjD 101.5 vs 103.0). Both teams play similar tempos and have comparable offensive outputs, but Miami's ACC-tested defensive rotations and coaching give them the slight edge in a game that comes down to late-game execution.

Purdue's AdjO of 133.3 is the best offense in the entire tournament field. A 35.5-point AdjEM gap (33.0 vs -2.5) is insurmountable. Queens' defense at 117.1 is the worst in the bracket — Purdue will score at will in a game that becomes a showcase for the Boilermakers' offensive system.

Michigan's AdjEM of 36.6 creates a 38.3-point gap over UMBC's -1.7. The Wolverines' defense (AdjD 91.0) is the second-best in the country, and their fast tempo (71.2) will generate transition scoring that UMBC's defense (AdjD 109.2) cannot handle. Michigan's complete statistical dominance makes this a wire-to-wire blowout.

Georgia holds a 2.3-point AdjEM edge (19.6 vs 17.3) driven by superior offense (AdjO 124.3 vs 119.6). Both teams play at fast tempos (72.0 vs 71.0), creating a high-possession game where Georgia's offensive efficiency advantage generates more scoring opportunities. The SEC's physicality gives Georgia the rebounding edge.

Texas Tech's AdjEM of 27.5 is 15.7 points above Akron's 11.8. The Red Raiders' offense (AdjO 126.3) creates a massive scoring gap that Akron's defense (AdjD 105.3) cannot close. Texas Tech's Big 12-tested defense (AdjD 98.8) handles mid-major offenses comfortably at this seed line.

Alabama's AdjEM of 26.7 creates an 18.5-point gap over Hofstra's 8.2. The Crimson Tide's explosive offense (AdjO 129.5) at the fastest tempo in the bracket (73.1) will overwhelm Hofstra's modest defense (AdjD 105.0). Alabama generates too many possessions for a 13-seed to keep pace.

Tennessee's AdjEM of 26.0 holds a 7.9-point edge over SMU's 18.1, driven by elite defense (AdjD 95.5 vs 105.0). Rick Barnes' teams thrive in tournament settings with their defensive identity. Tennessee's slower tempo (65.2) limits SMU's transition game and turns this into a half-court battle that favors the Volunteers' defensive pressure.

Virginia's AdjEM of 26.4 creates a 23.0-point gap over Wright State's 3.4. Tony Bennett's packline defense (AdjD 95.8) will strangle Wright State's limited offense (AdjO 112.4). Virginia's deliberate tempo (65.6) minimizes variance and turns this into a methodical defensive grinder.

Santa Clara's AdjEM of 21.3 actually exceeds Kentucky's 19.9, driven by superior offense (AdjO 125.0 vs 119.9). Kentucky's defense (AdjD 100.0) is a full 4 points worse than needed to contain Santa Clara's shooting. The Broncos are a classic mis-seeded 10 with the efficiency profile of a 5-seed — the numbers favor the upset.

Iowa State's AdjEM of 31.1 creates a 35.2-point gap over Tennessee State's -4.1. The Cyclones' elite defense (AdjD 92.6) is the foundation — Tennessee State's offense (AdjO 107.8) drops to nearly unplayable levels against this caliber of defense. T.J. Otzelberger's defensive system suffocates 15-seeds.

round_of_32.reasoning: Duke's AdjEM of 37.3 is 13.7 points above Ohio State's 23.6. While Ohio State's offense (AdjO 125.2) is respectable, Duke's defense (AdjD 90.8) will contain it, and Duke's own offense (AdjO 128.1) creates a scoring edge Ohio State's defense (AdjD 101.6) cannot match. Duke controls both ends of the floor.

Nearly identical AdjEM (St. John's 25.7, Kansas 23.5) but Kansas has the coaching edge with Bill Self in March. St. John's elite defense (AdjD 94.1) makes this a grinder, but Kansas's balanced efficiency profile and tournament pedigree at the 4-5 matchup line give the Jayhawks the edge in a close game.

Michigan State's AdjEM edge (26.7 vs 25.9) is slim, but Izzo's track record in R32 games is elite. Michigan State's defense (AdjD 96.2) matches up well against Louisville's high-volume offense. At similar tempos, Izzo's adjustments between rounds give Michigan State the tactical advantage.

UConn's AdjEM of 28.1 holds a 5.3-point edge over UCLA's 22.8. The Huskies' defense (AdjD 95.0) is better equipped to slow UCLA's offense (AdjO 124.5) than UCLA's defense (AdjD 101.8) is to contain UConn. Dan Hurley's tournament experience and UConn's championship pedigree are decisive in a 2-7 matchup.

Florida's AdjEM of 33.8 towers over Iowa's 21.9 — an 11.9-point gap. Florida's defense (AdjD 92.3) will suffocate Iowa's offense (AdjO 122.1), and Florida's own offense (AdjO 126.1) overwhelms Iowa's weaker defense (AdjD 100.2). The 1-seed's complete efficiency dominance controls this game from tip to finish.

Vanderbilt's AdjEM of 28.1 edges Nebraska's 23.8 by 4.3 points, driven by a massive offensive gap (AdjO 127.4 vs 117.3). Nebraska's elite defense (AdjD 93.5) is the best in this matchup, but Vanderbilt generates enough offensive volume at a faster tempo to overcome the defensive resistance.

Illinois' AdjEM of 33.7 is 12.3 points above North Carolina's 21.4. The Illini's offense (AdjO 131.9) is on another level — second-best in the field. UNC's defense (AdjD 100.1) cannot contain this scoring machine. Illinois' efficiency advantage on both ends makes this a convincing 3-seed advancement.

Houston's AdjEM of 33.0 is 14.5 points above Texas A&M's 18.5. Houston's defense (AdjD 92.4) will smother the Aggies' offense (AdjO 119.9), creating a half-court nightmare. Texas A&M's R64 upset magic fades against Houston's suffocating defensive system and slow-tempo control.

Arizona's AdjEM of 35.5 is 14.6 points above Utah State's 20.9. The Wildcats' elite defense (AdjD 91.4) will contain Utah State's strong offense (AdjO 123.0), and Arizona's own offensive firepower (AdjO 126.9) at a fast pace creates too many scoring opportunities. Arizona's complete profile overwhelms the upset-minded Aggies.

Arkansas's AdjEM (26.3) edges Wisconsin's (25.5) by a slim margin, but Arkansas's explosive offense (AdjO 127.9) at a fast tempo (71.0) overwhelms Wisconsin's slower pace preference (68.7). Wisconsin's defense is decent but porous by elite standards (AdjD 101.6), and Arkansas's transition attack exploits that weakness.

Gonzaga's AdjEM (26.3) edges BYU's (20.4) by 5.9 points with dramatically better defense (AdjD 94.0 vs 104.3). In a WCC rivalry matchup, Gonzaga's defensive advantage (10.3 points better) neutralizes BYU's offensive volume. Mark Few knows BYU's tendencies and will scheme to limit their shooters.

Purdue's AdjEM of 33.0 is 13.4 points above Miami's 19.6. Purdue's nation-best offense (AdjO 133.3) will exploit Miami's average defense (AdjD 101.5). Even at a slower tempo, Purdue's offensive efficiency per possession is too high for Miami to keep pace. The 2-seed's scoring machine rolls on.

Michigan's AdjEM of 36.6 is 17.0 points above Georgia's 19.6. The Wolverines' defense (AdjD 91.0) will lock down Georgia's offense (AdjO 124.3), and Michigan's own offense (AdjO 127.6) overwhelms Georgia's weak defense (AdjD 104.8). Michigan controls the tempo at 71.2 and dominates both ends of the floor.

Alabama's AdjEM (26.7) barely edges Texas Tech's (27.5) — this is essentially even. But Alabama's explosive offense (AdjO 129.5) at a blazing tempo (73.1) creates more possessions and scoring variance. Texas Tech's defense (AdjD 98.8) is solid but Alabama's pace pushes the game into a track meet that favors the Crimson Tide's firepower.

Virginia's AdjEM (26.4) edges Tennessee's (26.0) by a razor-thin margin. Both teams play elite defense at deliberate tempos — this becomes a classic low-possession grinder. Virginia's packline defense (AdjD 95.8) slightly outperforms Tennessee's (AdjD 95.5), and in a game with limited possessions, that marginal defensive edge decides it.

Iowa State's AdjEM of 31.1 is 9.8 points above Santa Clara's 21.3. The Cyclones' elite defense (AdjD 92.6) will disrupt Santa Clara's strong offense (AdjO 125.0). Santa Clara's R64 upset energy fades against Iowa State's defensive system and the 10-seed's lack of tournament depth experience.

sweet_16.reasoning: Duke's AdjEM of 37.3 is 13.8 points above Kansas's 23.5. Duke's generational offense (AdjO 128.1) overwhelms Kansas's solid defense (AdjD 94.4), and Duke's own defense (AdjD 90.8) contains Kansas's balanced attack. Bill Self's tournament coaching narrows the gap, but the statistical mismatch is too large.

UConn's AdjEM (28.1) edges Michigan State's (26.7) by 1.4 points. Both are elite defensive teams (AdjD 95.0 vs 96.2), making this a half-court slugfest. UConn's offensive edge (AdjO 123.1 vs 122.9) is razor-thin, but Dan Hurley's championship pedigree and UConn's ability to execute in late-game situations tip the scale.

Florida's AdjEM of 33.8 holds a 5.7-point edge over Vanderbilt's 28.1. Both are SEC teams with explosive offenses (AdjO 126.1 vs 127.4), but Florida's defense (AdjD 92.3 vs 99.3) is the clear separator — 7 points better. In an SEC rivalry Sweet 16 matchup, the team that gets stops advances, and Florida's defensive profile is significantly superior.

Houston's AdjEM (33.0) edges Illinois' (33.7) — essentially identical elite teams. However, Houston's historically elite defense (AdjD 92.4) matches up perfectly against Illinois' high-volume offense (AdjO 131.9). Houston's ability to slow the tempo and force half-court execution neutralizes Illinois' offensive rhythm. The Cougars' defensive identity is built for exactly this type of opponent.

Arizona's AdjEM of 35.5 is 9.2 points above Arkansas's 26.3. Both teams play at fast tempos (70.0 vs 71.0), creating a high-possession shootout — but Arizona is more efficient on both ends. Arizona's defense (AdjD 91.4 vs 101.7) is the decisive edge, containing Arkansas's explosive offense while Arizona's own attack (AdjO 126.9) creates separation.

Purdue's AdjEM (33.0) holds a 6.7-point edge over Gonzaga's (26.3), driven by the nation's best offense (AdjO 133.3 vs 120.3). Gonzaga's defense (AdjD 94.0) is strong, but Purdue's offensive system generates elite looks that even good defenses cannot fully contain. Purdue's size and half-court execution overwhelm Gonzaga in a slower-paced Sweet 16 game.

Michigan's AdjEM of 36.6 is 9.9 points above Alabama's 26.7. Michigan's defense (AdjD 91.0) is built to slow Alabama's blazing tempo (73.1) and force half-court execution. Alabama's explosive offense (AdjO 129.5) keeps it competitive, but Michigan's complete profile — elite defense and strong offense (AdjO 127.6) — is too balanced to overcome.

Iowa State's AdjEM (31.1) edges Virginia's (26.4) by 4.7 points. Both are elite defensive teams at slow tempos, but Iowa State's offensive efficiency (AdjO 123.8 vs 122.2) creates the margin. Virginia's packline limits Iowa State's transition game, but Iowa State's superior overall efficiency profile prevails in a grinding, low-scoring affair.

elite_8.reasoning: Duke's AdjEM of 37.3 holds a 9.2-point edge over UConn's 28.1. Duke's offense (AdjO 128.1) creates a massive scoring gap that UConn's defense (AdjD 95.0) can slow but cannot stop. In an Elite Eight matchup between two blue-blood programs, Duke's complete statistical superiority on both ends — offense and defense — is the difference.

Florida (AdjEM 33.8) vs Houston (33.0) — two elite teams separated by 0.8 points. Houston's defense (AdjD 92.4) is marginally better than Florida's (92.3), but Florida's offense (AdjO 126.1 vs 125.4) and faster tempo (70.4 vs 63.3) create more scoring opportunities. Florida's pace forces Houston out of their slow-grind comfort zone.

Arizona's AdjEM (35.5) edges Purdue's (33.0) by 2.5 points. Purdue has the better offense (AdjO 133.3 vs 126.9), but Arizona's defense (AdjD 91.4 vs 100.3) is dramatically superior — nearly 9 points better. In an Elite Eight game, defense wins. Arizona's ability to contain Purdue's elite offense while generating efficient scoring of their own is the decider.

Michigan's AdjEM of 36.6 holds a 5.5-point edge over Iowa State's 31.1. Both have elite defenses (AdjD 91.0 vs 92.6), but Michigan's offense (AdjO 127.6 vs 123.8) is the separator. Michigan's faster tempo (71.2 vs 67.0) creates extra possessions that compound their efficiency advantage. The Wolverines' complete profile advances.

final_four.reasoning: Arizona (AdjEM 35.5) vs Florida (33.8) — a 1.7-point edge for Arizona. Both are 1-seeds with elite complete profiles, but Arizona's defense (AdjD 91.4 vs 92.3) is the marginal difference. In a Final Four coin-flip between two powerhouses, Arizona's defensive edge and Tommy Lloyd's tactical adjustments are the tiebreaker.

Duke (AdjEM 37.3) vs Michigan (36.6) — the two best teams in the country by efficiency margin. Duke's offense (AdjO 128.1 vs 127.6) holds a slim edge, but the real difference is Michigan's defense (AdjD 91.0) is slightly better than Duke's (AdjD 90.8). This is the ultimate coin flip, but Duke's offensive ceiling and Jon Scheyer's tournament coaching lift them in a tight finish.

championship.reasoning: Duke (AdjEM 37.3) vs Arizona (35.5) — a 1.8-point efficiency edge for the Blue Devils. Duke's offense (AdjO 128.1) is the best scoring machine in the tournament, and their defense (AdjD 90.8) matches Arizona's elite standard (91.4). In a championship game between two 1-seeds, Duke's marginally superior efficiency on both ends and their offensive ceiling in a high-stakes setting give them the title.
